abstract: Creates a new ACL entry with the given characteristics, and adds it to an access instance.

declaration.code: func SecACLCreateWithSimpleContents(_ access: SecAccess, _ applicationList: CFArray?, _ description: CFString, _ promptSelector: SecKeychainPromptSelector, _ newAcl: UnsafeMutablePointer<SecACL?>) -> OSStatus

overview:
## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

The ACL entry returned by this method includes a list of trusted apps, the name of the keychain item as it appears in user prompts, the prompt selector flag, and a list of one or more operations to which this ACL entry applies. By default, a new ACL entry applies to all operations. Use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecACLUpdateAuthorizations(_:_:)] method to set the list of operations for an ACL entry.

The system requires exactly one owner ACL entry in each access instance. The [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecACLCreateWithSimpleContents(_:_:_:_:_:)] method fails if you attempt to add a second owner entry. To change owner access controls, use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecAccessCopyMatchingACLList(_:_:)] function to find the owner entry (the only one with an authorization tag of [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecACLAuthorizationChangeACL]) and the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecACLSetContents(_:_:_:_:)] method to change it as needed.

# SecACLCreateWithSimpleContents(_:_:_:_:_:)

**Function**

Creates a new ACL entry with the given characteristics, and adds it to an access instance.

## Declaration

```swift
func SecACLCreateWithSimpleContents(_ access: SecAccess, _ applicationList: CFArray?, _ description: CFString, _ promptSelector: SecKeychainPromptSelector, _ newAcl: UnsafeMutablePointer<SecACL?>) -> OSStatus
```

## Parameters

- **access**: The access instance to which to add the information.
- **applicationList**: An array of [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrustedApplication] instances identifying apps that are allowed access to the keychain item without user confirmation.

Set this parameter to `nil` to indicate that any app can use this item. Pass an empty array to indicate that there are no trusted apps.
- **description**: The human readable name to be used to refer to this item when the user is prompted.
- **promptSelector**: A set of prompt selector flags. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainPromptSelector] for possible values.
- **newAcl**: A pointer the method uses to return the new [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecACL] instance.

## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

The ACL entry returned by this method includes a list of trusted apps, the name of the keychain item as it appears in user prompts, the prompt selector flag, and a list of one or more operations to which this ACL entry applies. By default, a new ACL entry applies to all operations. Use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecACLUpdateAuthorizations(_:_:)] method to set the list of operations for an ACL entry.



The system requires exactly one owner ACL entry in each access instance. The [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecACLCreateWithSimpleContents(_:_:_:_:_:)] method fails if you attempt to add a second owner entry. To change owner access controls, use the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecAccessCopyMatchingACLList(_:_:)] function to find the owner entry (the only one with an authorization tag of [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecACLAuthorizationChangeACL]) and the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecACLSetContents(_:_:_:_:)] method to change it as needed.
